text: The video shows deep infiltrating endometriosis with visible black, white, and red lesions located posterior to the uterus. The uterus is moving, and both ovaries and fallopian tubes are visible on the left and right sides.

The surgeon lifts and elevates a nodule from the left pelvic sidewall, progressively dissecting it off. The yellow tissue indicates the beginning of the bowel at the margin where the cervix meets the rectum. Hemorrhagic collections associated with endometriosis are visible, along with old hemorrhage appearing as black areas. The surgical device cuts through some fat, eliminating lesions.

The surgeon removes a large nodule of endometriosis from the left side, followed by the excision of another nodule on the right side. The nodules are visibly excised from the deep fibrotic tissue in the pelvic region.
